briefdescription.para: \par \b Purpose\/ \: Define the TreeNode data structure — recursive tree representation of document structure with annotation-aware construction, merging, and multi-page tracking.

detaileddescription.para: \par \b Scope\/ \: Document tree: nodes with text, annotations, hierarchy metadata, parent/child links, tree construction from lines, annotation merging. \par \b Input\/ \: Node ID, text, annotations, metadata, children list, parent reference. \par \b Output\/ \: TreeNode instances forming a complete document tree. \par \b Links\/ \: [INHERITS(5): Serializable, USES_API(8): ApiTreeNode, READS_DATA_FROM(9): LineWithMeta, LineMetadata, HierarchyLevel, Annotation, AnnotationMerger] \par \b Invariants\/ \:

listitem.para: Root node has parent=None and node_id="0"

detaileddescription.para: Q: Why a tree rather than a flat list? A: Documents have inherent hierarchical structure (sections, subsections, lists). A tree preserves this structure, enabling semantic navigation and table-of-contents generation. \par \b Changes\/ \:
LAST_CHANGE: [v1.0.0 – Added semantic markup and LDD logging] \par \b Module Map\/ \:

CLASS 10[Document tree node] => TreeNode METHOD 8[Create root from lines] => create METHOD 7[Add child node] => add_child METHOD 7[Append text to node] => add_text METHOD 6[Get tree root] => get_root METHOD 8[Merge all annotations] => merge_annotations METHOD 8[API schema conversion] => to_api_schema \par \b Use Cases\/ \:
